title: BALI Nat Conf 2025: Why the Landscape Industry Will Outperform the UK Economy | Trevor Williams
description:
The UK economy faces slow growth, higher costs, geopolitical uncertainty and persistent skills shortages. But the outlook for the landscape industry is significantly more positive.

In this keynote, leading economist Professor Jonathan Portes examines the global and UK economic forces shaping the next decade and explains why landscaping, green infrastructure and climate-resilient development are likely to outperform the wider economy.

He explores how climate change, population shifts, technological advancement, housing demand and regulation are creating long-term opportunities for landscape businesses of every size.

Topics covered include:

• The economic outlook for the UK and the effect of global uncertainty
• Why inflation, borrowing costs and wage pressures are likely to remain challenging
• How climate resilience is driving demand for landscape services
• The importance of green spaces to urban wellbeing and public health
• Opportunities created by housing development, biodiversity and water management
• How automation, robotics, drones, sensors and AI can improve productivity
• Why specialist expertise and long-term contracts can strengthen margins
• The continuing need to invest in skills, training and recruitment
• How data-led decision-making can help businesses compete and grow

The keynote argues that landscaping is no longer a discretionary service. As towns and cities adapt to climate change, green infrastructure, biodiversity, sustainable water management and resilient public spaces will become increasingly essential.

While the UK economy may grow slowly, demand for the products and expertise provided by the landscape industry is expected to rise.

The conclusion is clear: climate resilience plus biodiversity creates a strong foundation for long-term growth, but success will depend on people, technology and a sound business strategy.

This keynote was recorded at the BALI National Conference, focused on climate resilience and future-proofing the landscape industry.

Find out more about the British Association of Landscape Industries at bali.org.uk.

title: BALI Nat Conf 2025 Sir Tim Smit keynote: Landscape Is a Life-Support System
description:
Landscape architecture is not a decorative finishing touch. It shapes human health, identity, belonging and the places in which people live their lives.

In this provocative and wide-ranging keynote, Sir Tim Smit, co-founder of the Eden Project, challenges landscape professionals to be more ambitious, more vocal and more confident about the value of their work.

Drawing on ideas from soil science, ecology, music, architecture, language and placemaking, Sir Tim explores the deep relationship between people and the natural world. He argues that landscape architects and landscape professionals should see themselves not simply as designers or contractors, but as champions for public health, citizenship and human wellbeing.

During the keynote, Sir Tim considers:

• Why people instinctively respond to natural environments
• The growing scientific evidence linking nature and human health
• Why landscape professionals deserve a stronger voice in major developments
• How buildings and public spaces affect emotion and wellbeing
• The importance of helping people learn to “read” the natural world
• Why language, poetry and storytelling can transform our relationship with place
• The need to move beyond describing landscape as an amenity or a “nice to have”
• How the industry can become a more powerful advocate for people, place and nature

His central challenge to the profession is clear: be braver about the importance of landscape. The spaces created by landscape professionals become places where people meet, fall in love, grieve, celebrate and build memories.

Landscape is not simply part of the built environment. It is part of the health service, the social fabric and the life-support system on which communities depend.

This keynote was recorded at the BALI National Conference, focused on climate resilience and future-proofing the landscape industry.

Find out more about the British Association of Landscape Industries at bali.org.uk.

title: BALI Nat Conf 2025 Helen Nyul keynote: BNG: The Biggest Opportunity for the Landscaping Industry
description:
Biodiversity Net Gain is not simply another regulatory burden. It represents a major commercial opportunity for landscape professionals who are ready to embrace it.

In this presentation, Helen Nyul, Director of Ecology at Verna, explains how landscape businesses can benefit from the growing demand for skilled biodiversity design, delivery and long-term habitat management.

Drawing on her experience as Head of Biodiversity at Barratt Redrow, Helen shares the story of Kingsbrook, a 2,400-home development created in partnership with the RSPB. The project demonstrated that development and nature do not need to be in conflict, delivering measurable wildlife improvements alongside financial savings and commercial success.

Helen explores:

• Why Biodiversity Net Gain creates opportunities for 30-year management contracts
• How climate resilience and biodiversity can be designed together
• The importance of involving ecologists and landscape professionals from the outset
• How businesses can develop the skills needed to deliver and maintain habitats
• Why collaboration, training and ecological expertise will provide a competitive advantage
• How nature-positive development can reduce costs, strengthen reputations and attract talent

The message is clear: businesses that invest now in understanding and delivering Biodiversity Net Gain will be better placed to win contracts, build long-term client relationships and help shape the future of the landscaping industry.

This presentation was recorded at the BALI National Conference, focused on climate resilience and future-proofing the landscape industry.

Find out more about the British Association of Landscape Industries and the support available to landscape professionals at bali.org.uk.

title: Behind the build Part 3: The BALI Hive at Chelsea: Collaboration, Climate Resilience and Community
description:
The BALI Hive Garden demonstrated how much can be achieved when landscape professionals come together with a shared purpose.

In the third and final film in this series, BALI members and industry leaders reflect on the completed garden at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show, the collaboration behind it and the important conversations it helped to inspire.

Designed for a compact space, the garden brought together climate-resilient planting, permeable paving, water management, solar power and a living wall to show how attractive, sustainable green spaces can be created in gardens, public spaces, high streets and balconies.

The film explores:

• How BALI members collaborated to bring the garden to life
• The value of sharing ideas, expertise and experience
• Why professional networks create a sense of community and belonging
• How small spaces can support biodiversity and climate resilience
• The role of landscaping in clean air, water management and urban greening
• Why green infrastructure matters to developers, designers, policymakers and communities
• How Chelsea created a platform for conversations about the future of the industry

The BALI Hive Garden was a living example of what BALI does best: bringing people together, supporting collaboration and helping the landscape industry demonstrate its value.

It showed that even a relatively small space can deliver meaningful environmental benefits while creating somewhere beautiful for people to enjoy.

This is the final video in a three-part series documenting the development and delivery of the BALI Hive Garden at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

Find out more about the British Association of Landscape Industries at bali.org.uk.

title: Behind the Build Part 2: Collaboration at the BALI Garden | RHS Chelsea | BALI x ProLandscaper
description:
In the second of our three-part video series with ProLandscaper, we go behind the scenes of the BALI garden build at RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

This episode explores the collaboration, skill and teamwork that bring Chelsea gardens to life, from designers, contractors, growers and suppliers to volunteers, students and industry leaders.

During build week, students from Capel Manor College joined the BALI garden team, gaining first-hand experience of RHS Chelsea and seeing how the industry comes together to create something extraordinary.

Featuring contributions from Mark Gregory, Jamie Gault, CEO of Perennial, and Helen Wadding from Perennial, this video celebrates the people, partnerships and shared passion behind one of horticulture’s most iconic events.

Thank you to ProLandscaper for producing the series.

title: Behind the Build Part 1: The Hive at RHS Chelsea 2026 | BALI x ProLandscaper
description:
The Hive was BALI's garden at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026 - and this three-part film, produced with ProLandscaper, follows it from empty plot to finished build.

Across the series, you'll see the groundworks go in, the planting take shape, and the team behind it talk through what it took to deliver a show garden under Chelsea conditions. It's a look at the craft, coordination and collaboration that BALI members bring to one of the most demanding stages in horticulture.

Garden designed by Matt Evans (Garden Room Living / Landscape Studios) and built by Frosts Landscaping, led by Alistair Bayford. With contributions from Mark Gregory and BALI Past President Adam White.
Suppliers and partners:
Millboard · Ca' Pietra · Creepers · Hillier Trees · Sky Garden · Furnitubes · Landscapeplus · Bourne Amenity · Land Technology
Film produced in collaboration with ProLandscaper.
The British Association of Landscape Industries (BALI) represents the professionals shaping the UK's landscaping and garden design industry. Find out more about BALI membership: www.bali.org.uk

title: BALI National Landscape Awards
description:
Welcome to the largest landscaping Awards in Europe

The National Landscape Awards provides the industry with a platform in which to showcase and improve their business and celebrate their achievements both within and outside of the industry.

Entry into the National Landscape Awards is limited to members of the British Association of Landscape Industries.

Enquire today regarding membership to ensure your company is eligible to enter!

The British Association of Landscape Industries is the leading Trade Association representing all landscape professionals - from design, build and maintenance through to supply, training and education. Over 900 Accredited members represent some of the best landscaping businesses across the country, from large corporations, to local traders, to individual designers.

Explore the opportunities Association membership could provide to your business!

#Awards #Landscaping

title: BALI National Awards Entry Workshop 2025
description: Join Chair of the Judging Panel, John Melmoe and Awards Judge Jason Lock as they delve into practical steps to make your entry submission stand out!
